title: Ranunculus commemorans
intro: Ranunculus commemorans, also known as the commemorative buttercup, is a species of flowering plant in the ranunculaceae family, characterized by its bright yellow petals and delicate appearance. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, boost the immune system, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Ranunculus commemorans include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are used to treat a range of ailments, from skin conditions and wounds to respiratory issues and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Ranunculus commemorans is classified within the family Ranunculaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Ranunculus. The exact classification and details of this specific species are not well-documented.

side_effects: Ranunculus commemorans is known to be highly toxic and contains a compound called ranunculin, which can cause severe side effects. Ingestion of the plant can lead to oral and gastrointestinal irritation, and can also cause numbness, tingling, and burning sensations in the mouth and throat. The toxic compound can also be absorbed through the skin, causing symptoms such as redness, itching, and blistering. If ingested in large quantities, Ranunculus commemorans can cause more severe symptoms, including difficulty breathing, abdominal pain, and even kidney damage. It is essential to exercise extreme caution when handling the plant or being around areas where it grows.
